exemplar.title:
How does varying the density of water, from 1000 kg m-3 to 1333 kg
m-3 by adding salt of mass 0kg, 0.5kg, 1kg, 1.5kg, 2kg to 6 litres of water, used to move a Pelton turbine, effect the Potential Difference generated over a period of 2 minutes?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does varying the density of water, from 1000 kg m-3 to 1333 kg
m-3 by adding salt of mass 0kg, 0.5kg, 1kg, 1.5kg, 2kg to 6 litres of water, used to move a Pelton turbine, effect the Potential Difference generated over a period of 2 minutes?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, consistent communication of topic and research question throughout the report
Verbatim reproduction and repeated focus on the research question ensures strong alignment between question and analysis
weaknesses: Minor lapses in wording (e.g. effect/affect confusion) and occasional informal narrative
Some methodological details (head height, actual volume measurements) remain unquantified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed the work firmly in the 5-6 band for clarity and focus, with very minor lapses preventing the top mark. Methodology was mostly complete and appropriate, warranting the upper half of the band. A holistic score of 5 accurately reflects these strengths and small imperfections.

strengths: Correct and relevant application of core physics concepts (Bernoulli, Faradayâs law, angular momentum)
Generally accurate use of specialist terminology and symbols
weaknesses: Several derivations contain conceptual or algebraic inaccuracies
Inconsistent use of terms (e.g. emf vs potential difference) and occasional notation slips
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates good subject knowledge with mostly relevant source application, but conceptual errors in key equations and inconsistent terminology constrain the work to the 3â4 band. A score of 4 fairly represents both the competence and the noted flaws.

strengths: Data is clearly processed with uncertainty propagation and relevant graphical analysis (steadyâflow window selection, RÂ² reporting)
Well-structured argument linking theory, hypothesis, and empirical evidence
Identification of limitations and suggestions for improvement show genuine critical engagement
weaknesses: Statistical treatment lacks advanced tests (e.g. significance, confidence intervals)
Evaluation of alternative explanations and error sources is present but not consistently deep
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work sits solidly in the 7â9 band: analysis and argumentation are coherent and directly relevant to the research question, and critical evaluation is evident. While missing the full sophistication of the top band, the reportâs consistent analytical quality justifies a high-end 9.

strengths: All structural conventions are met (title page, TOC, pagination, word count) and figures/tables are captioned
Visual elements generally support communication, with error bars and embedded images
weaknesses: Figure labels occasionally overlap text and styling is inconsistent (mix of fonts, borders)
Equation numbering is irregular and some captions are not explicitly referenced in the text
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although minor formatting inconsistencies exist, the report fully satisfies the criteria for the top band of Presentation. The clarity and completeness of structure, alongside effective layout, support awarding the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Specialist terms such as âheadâ, âPelton turbineâ, âimpulse turbineâ, âpotential differenceâ, âpercentage uncertaintyâ and symbols (Ï, Ï, Î¦) are employed correctly in most instances.  Occasional slipsâusing âemfâ and âpotential differenceâ interchangeably, spelling/grammar lapses, and the stray âQâ for Î©âshow that usage is not fully consistent.  Nonetheless, the vocabulary is appropriate for IB Physics and usually accurate, meeting the expectations for the 3â4 band at the upper end.

The argument follows a logical path: theoretical expectation â hypothesis â measurement â graphical evidence â conclusion and evaluation.  Links to literature and equations are used to justify claims, and counter-arguments (minimal effect of density, economic feasibility) are considered.  Some reasoning steps are oversimplified (density assumed sole variable) and quantitative depth is modest, preventing the sophistication needed for the 7â9 band, but overall coherence and development warrant a high score within 4â6.

The methodology is described step-by-step, lists independent/controlled variables, includes a circuit diagram, uncertainty treatment, and repeats each density three times.  Instrument choice (Vernier probe, Arduino flow sensor) shows informed selection, and the student addresses uncontrolled flow-rate with a work-around.  Nevertheless, head height, salinity measurement, and turbine loading are not quantified; the use of reused water and assumption of constant volume introduce uncontrolled variation; and some equations used to justify design contain errors.  These gaps place the work solidly in the âmostly complete with generally relevant methodsâ descriptor, meriting the top of the 3â4 band.

The student applies core physics ideasâgravitational potential energy, Bernoulli style energy balance, angular momentum, Faradayâs lawâand attempts to link them to the experiment.  A reasonable qualitative explanation for the relationship between density, flow rate, angular speed and emf is given, and literature sources are cited.  However, several derivations contain conceptual or algebraic inaccuracies (e.g. Equation 1 with duplicated height terms, substitution of density without cancelling volume, incorrect final emf expression).  These errors reduce coherence but do not erase overall understanding, fitting the âgood knowledge with mostly relevant source applicationâ description (score 4).

The student discusses sources of uncertainty, flow-rate problems, the limited density range, and practical/ecological implications of using salt water.  Suggestions for improvements (alternative turbines, pressure studies) are made.  However, evaluation lacks depth in error quantification or alternative explanations (e.g. electrical loading, salinity effects on conductivity).  Critical reflection is present but not throughout, fitting the lower end of the 4â6 band (score 4).

The student states the title, research question and purpose prominently on the first page, repeats the question in the introduction, and re-phrases it when discussing the hypothesis. All components of the topic (water density, Pelton turbine, two-minute interval, potential difference) are explicitly mentioned and remain visible throughout the table of contents and later discussion.  The reader can clearly understand what is being investigated and why it matters (possible optimisation of hydropower).  Minor lapses in wording (e.g. âeffectâ instead of âaffectâ) and some informal narrative (Percy Jackson) do not significantly impede clarity.  Overall communication is accurate and purposeful but not polished enough for the top of the band, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

Tables, graphs and photographs are appropriately embedded and captioned; the two key graphs aid interpretation and error bars are included.  Overall visual layout is coherent and aids communication.  Resolution of some images is low and there is inconsistent styling (mix of serif/sans-serif, extra borders), but these issues are minor.  Hence the strand meets the criteria for effective layout, warranting a score of 3 within the top band.

The research question is reproduced verbatim, is sharply delimited (density range, salt masses, fixed water volume, 2-minute period) and is consistently referred to in data processing, graphs and the conclusion.  The discussion stays centred on the same dependent variable (potential difference) and explicitly tests the hypothesis drawn from Equation 5, demonstrating strong alignment.  While the question is arguably more technology-than-physics focused, its formulation and focus meet the descriptors for a high level.  Slight ambiguity about whether density truly is an independent variable (noted in evaluation) prevents full marks, so 5 is awarded.

Data are organised in clearly labelled tables, processed into averages with propagated uncertainties, and represented in time-series and density plots.  The student identifies the steady-flow window (20â100 s) using flow-sensor evidence, selects those points for analysis, and derives a best-fit line with RÂ².  Errors and limitations are noted.  While the statistical treatment is basic (no gradient uncertainty, no significance test), the analysis remains clearly relevant and more than adequate, matching the middle of the 4â6 band.

The essay contains all required structural elements: title page with word count, table of contents, section headings, labelled figures and tables, references and appendix.  Pagination is present and within the 4000-word limit.  Some figure labels overlap text, equation numbering is inconsistent, and minor typographical artefacts appear, so the work does not fully exemplify âclear and appropriateâ at the highest level, but it comfortably meets the descriptor for 3â4 (score 3).

annotations.comment: Density calculation table shows values, but separate the header row for clarity and add uncertainty column.
The title is clearly stated at the start, meeting IB requirements and orienting the reader effectively.
The research question is reproduced verbatim, providing clear focus. However, consider refining phrasing to avoid âaffectâ vs âeffectâ confusion.
Word count is provided on the title page, satisfying the formatting requirement.
The table of contents is comprehensive and correctly formatted, aiding navigation.
Consider reducing indentation inconsistency in the TOC entries to improve visual coherence.
Opening narrative uses informal Percy Jackson analogy, which distracts from scientific tone. Tighten language to maintain academic focus.
Figure 1 is well captioned, but reference it explicitly in text to integrate visual evidence with discussion.
The energy equation is presented correctly, but variables m, v, and h are not defined here. Add definitions to aid reader comprehension.
The derivation in Equation 1 duplicates height terms (h and hÂ²). Verify algebraic consistency and correct annotation.
Specialist terms like âdensityâ and âmagnetic fluxâ are used accurately, demonstrating good concept application.
Equation 5 contains a misplaced h term in the square root. Revisit substitution steps from Equations 2 and 4.
Logical flow from magnetic flux to emf formula is clear; theoretical framework supports experimental design.
In the inertia derivation, canceling m and r is correct, but clarify each algebraic step with brief commentary.
Assuming cylindrical shape for the turbine simplifies I but consider noting any deviation from ideal shape as potential error.
The five Equations are numbered inconsistently; adopt a consistent numbering style throughout.
Descriptions of impulse vs reaction turbines are accurate and demonstrate good subject knowledge.
The hypothesis states density should not affect emf, but this contradicts your own theoretical derivation predicting a small effect. Clarify this tension.
Overview correctly recaps experimental purpose, but repeat of hypothesis wording could be avoided in this section.
Controlled variable table is complete, showing clear rationale and method for each item.
Uncontrolled variable flow rate is identified, but quantify how Arduino readings translate to volumetric rate to strengthen methodology.
Procedure steps are clearly enumerated; consider adding timing precision and calibration details for reproducibility.
Figure 5 and 6 images of setup are wellâchosen. Including scale bars would further clarify physical dimensions.
Circuit diagram (Figure 7) is included but lacks component labels. Annotate resistor, probe, and flow sensor explicitly.
Uncertainty propagation is handled correctly, demonstrating solid quantitative treatment.
Assuming constant volume for saline water needs empirical support; consider measuring final volume after mixing.
